response.justification: Maria is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine developer-tooling interest — she worked on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments and has authored test runners and assertion libraries — but her core stack (TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, PHP) does not match the role's hard requirements. She shows no Kotlin experience, no Spring or enterprise Java framework background, and only incidental Java usage, making her a poor fit for a JVM-centric enterprise IDE position despite solid general seniority.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and authored language tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development
Broad polyglot experience across web, mobile, desktop, and low-latency systems
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only minor, dated Java exposure; lacks deep JVM expertise
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise framework / application server background
Recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js product engineering, far from JVM IDE tooling

key_factors.factor: IDE internals and language tooling background
